scenario_results.evidence: Fresh agent answered directly in one sentence with no delegation, repository command, or write.
Fresh agent diagnosed duplicated guidance trees read-only and made no changes.
Fresh independent audit remained read-only and returned evidence-backed findings.
Fresh agent derived 37 unit jobs, one synthesis, conditional verifiers, and visible capacity waves without truncation.

Fresh regression explained four logical tickets separately from one live agent and reported requested/effective model and effort, routing reasons, inheritance, fallback, and unobservable values without writes or tests.

Fresh Sol verifier produced two byte-identical read-only plans whose five exact writes matched content hashes, applied only the confirmed paths, preserved the CRLF AGENTS.md prefix byte-for-byte, proved a second apply was a byte-and-mtime no-op, and obtained doctor PASSED exit 0.

Fresh Sol verifier proved stale-plan drift returned CONFLICT exit 2 with zero writes; after explicit resolution, only config, manifest, one canonical reviewer, and its Codex/Claude projections changed, unknown config keys survived, and the doctor passed.
Fresh forward test resolved precedence, eight logical jobs, four operational waves, safety-cap behavior, visibility, and model routing without writes or delegation.

Both live disposable 44-case harnesses matched every expected status and exit code, including reasoning-policy validation, incomplete manifests, exact model IDs, portable rule paths, control-file reparse rejection, routing, casing, adapter semantics, and managed-path boundaries.

Both live 44-case harnesses rejected managed-path and control-file reparse traversal before target access; the configurator corpus also rejected nested and top-level Claude root candidates before selection, decoding, hashing, or writes.
Both live harnesses rejected missing, contrary, and mismatched canonical adapter semantics despite refreshed managed byte hashes.

Both 44-case doctor corpora rejected malformed reasoning policy and effort ordering, config keys, non-portable artifact paths, role IDs, and newline-terminated model IDs; the dedicated contract casing corpus is tracked separately.

Fresh regression rendered the 0.5 flow fixture read-only in Italian with real job IDs, responsibility, live agent, requested and effective models and efforts, routing reason or inheritance, blocker, completion criterion, and next action without invented runtime facts.

Fresh agent detected an uninitialized project, generated deterministic baseline plan 0b41e1109ff02c4f894f6aa98dec23cbcd5891cb866e017b786c1a11458bec86 with the bundled Codex Python runtime, made no project writes, asked for one confirmation, and preserved the original task for automatic resumption.

Fresh Terra low contexts proved the 0.5.2 modes: bare and brief rendered the chat-friendly structure read-only; a raw focused run exposed Flow-selection interference, explicit Help precedence was added to all non-Help skills, a fresh source rerun returned focused Flow help without live state, and the final exact one-H1 default prompt produced the complete required layout.

Fresh Terra low agent routed four jobs to Terra low, Terra high, Sol xhigh, and independent Sol xhigh, separated new-task Sol medium guidance from inherited active state, and planned capacity-aware waves without execution or global changes.

fixture_results.evidence: Python doctor passed the valid corpus case with exit code 0.
PowerShell doctor passed the valid corpus case with exit code 0.
Python doctor detected managed drift with exit code 2.
PowerShell doctor detected managed drift with exit code 2.
Python doctor rejected both empty-manifest and omitted-config with exit code 1 using the canonical incomplete-fixture command template.
PowerShell doctor rejected both empty-manifest and omitted-config with exit code 1 using the canonical incomplete-fixture command template.
Python doctor rejected the reparse boundary before target access with exit code 1.
PowerShell doctor rejected the reparse boundary before target access with exit code 1.
Python doctor rejected the incorrect canonical marker despite a matching managed hash, with exit code 1.
PowerShell doctor rejected the incorrect canonical marker despite a matching managed hash, with exit code 1.

validation_results.evidence: Official skill-creator validator passed after the control, doctor, and payload-gate edits.
Official skill-creator validator passed for the chat-friendly Help layout and explicit command-precedence contract.
Official skill-creator validator passed after the control, doctor, and payload-gate edits.
Official skill-creator validator passed after the control, doctor, and payload-gate edits.
Official skill-creator validator passed for the new read-only flow skill.
Official skill-creator validator passed after the control, doctor, and payload-gate edits.
Official plugin-creator validator passed after the control, doctor, and payload-gate edits.

Python accepted the 0.5.2 Markdown layout, explicit Help precedence, semantic guide, prompt values, payload and trace bindings, then completed its live doctor corpus with only the independent audit pending.
PowerShell independently accepted the same 0.5.2 contract, payload and trace bindings, then completed its live doctor corpus with only the independent audit pending.

Expanded disposable-copy corpus completed 34/34 matches with zero unavailable runtimes. Python and PowerShell both rejected a missing H1, broken quick/model/ticket tables, command fields, inline examples, Help precedence, routing semantics, and weakened prompt values.

Disposable configurator corpus passed 8/8 cases covering exact and idempotent plans, conflicts, drift and reparse safety including both Claude root candidates before read, rich adapters, unknown-key preservation, localized edits, and rollback.

audit_results.evidence: Independent Sol xhigh review found one validator-coverage gap for the required H1. After repair, re-audit verified the one-H1 gate, 34/34 adversarial corpus, final exact-default-prompt evaluation, routing precedence, Python/PowerShell parity, and all release hashes; no HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW findings remain.
